page_content: Chemistry Major, Fall Junior Year (14 Credits) CHEM 481 (3 credits) A course primarily for chemists and Physical Chemistry I chemical engineers. CHEM 483 (2 credits) One hour of lecture-recitation and one Physical Chemistry three-hour laboratory period per week. An Laboratory I introduction to the principles and application of quantitative techniques in physical chemical measurements. Experiments will be coordinated with topics in CHEM 481. ENGL 39X (3 credits) Junior English GenEd (3 credits) Elective (3 credits) Chemistry Major, Spring Junior Year (15 Credits) CHEM 482 (3 credits) A course primarily for chemists and Physical Chemistry II chemical engineers. CHEM 484 (2 credits) One hour lecture-recitation and one three- Physical Chemistry hour laboratory period per week. A Laboratory II continuation of CHEM 483. Advanced quantitative techniques necessary in physical chemical measurements. Experiments will be coordinated with topics in CHEM 482. CHEM 395 (1 credit) Junior standing. Seminar on professional Professional Issues in issues. Professional responsibilities, ethics, Chemistry and interview techniques, career opportunities, Biochemistry graduate/professional school, race and gender issues. (Spring semester only.) GenEd (3 credits) Electives (6 credits)
